name: Ascend SQLite Database Layer
description: SQLite database layer for the Ascend engineering management CLI. Provides connection management, schema initialization with versioning, and health checks for a database storing teams, members, meetings, goals, performance snapshots, coaching entries, and schedules. Implements full-text search on meeting transcripts using FTS5.

types.name: Row
types.description: sqlite3.Row - row factory for dict-like access to query results

types.name: HealthCheckResult

fields.name: ok
fields.description: True if database is healthy, False otherwise

fields.name: error
fields.description: Error message if ok is False

fields.name: version
fields.description: Schema version number if ok is True

fields.name: tables
fields.description: Dictionary mapping table names to row counts if ok is True

types.description: Dictionary returned by check_db with health status

functions.name: get_connection
functions.description: Opens a SQLite connection with Write-Ahead Logging (WAL) mode enabled and foreign key constraints enforced. Sets row_factory to sqlite3.Row for dict-like access to query results.

inputs.name: db_path
inputs.description: Filesystem path to the database file

error_cases.name: file_access_error
error_cases.condition: db_path directory doesn't exist or insufficient permissions

error_cases.name: invalid_path
error_cases.condition: db_path cannot be converted to string

preconditions: db_path is a valid Path object
postconditions: Connection has WAL journal mode enabled
Connection has foreign key enforcement enabled
Connection row_factory is set to sqlite3.Row
side_effects: Creates database file if it doesn't exist

structured_side_effects.description: Creates database file if it doesn't exist

functions.name: init_db
functions.description: Creates the database file (if needed), applies the schema if not present or outdated, and returns an open connection. Checks current schema version and only applies schema updates if current version is less than SCHEMA_VERSION (2). Creates parent directories if they don't exist.

inputs.name: db_path
inputs.description: Filesystem path to the database file

error_cases.name: permission_error
error_cases.condition: Insufficient permissions to create directories or database file

error_cases.name: sql_error
error_cases.condition: Invalid SQL in _SCHEMA_SQL or execution failure

error_cases.name: operational_error
error_cases.condition: Database is locked or corrupted during schema application

preconditions: db_path is a valid Path object
postconditions: All parent directories of db_path exist
schema_version table has entry for SCHEMA_VERSION
All tables, triggers, and FTS5 virtual tables are created
Returns open connection with WAL mode and foreign keys enabled
side_effects: Creates database file if it doesn't exist
Executes schema SQL script if version is outdated
Commits transaction if schema is applied

structured_side_effects.description: Applies schema SQL script and version record

functions.name: check_db
functions.description: Performs health check on the database by verifying existence, schema initialization, and returning table row counts. Returns a dictionary with ok status, version, and table counts. If database doesn't exist or schema is uninitialized, returns error state.

inputs.name: db_path
inputs.description: Filesystem path to the database file

error_cases.name: sql_error
error_cases.condition: Query execution fails on table count (table doesn't exist)

preconditions: db_path is a valid Path object
postconditions: If ok=False, includes 'error' key with description
side_effects: Executes SELECT queries on schema_version and all tables

invariants: _SCHEMA_SQL contains CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS statements for: schema_version, teams, members, member_flags, team_members, meetings, meeting_items, goals, performance_snapshots, coaching_entries, schedules
_SCHEMA_SQL creates FTS5 virtual table meetings_fts for full-text search
_SCHEMA_SQL creates triggers (meetings_ai, meetings_ad, meetings_au) to sync meetings table with meetings_fts
All connections use WAL journal mode
All connections have foreign key constraints enabled
All connections use sqlite3.Row as row_factory
check_db expects tables: members, member_flags, teams, team_members, meetings, meeting_items, goals, performance_snapshots, coaching_entries, schedules

data_access.rationale: This module manages persistent storage for the Ascend engineering management application using SQLite. It creates, reads, and manages database schema and connections.

side_effects.rationale: Creates SQLite database files and parent directories as needed
Opens and reads SQLite database files for connection and health checks
Applies database schema with tables, triggers, and FTS5 virtual tables when initializing or upgrading

authority.rationale: This module is the authoritative source for SQLite connection configuration, schema initialization and versioning, and database health status for the Ascend application
